description: This image is a 3D rendering of the Earth from space, illustrating satellite data acquisition coverage, likely for Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR). The Earth, showing visible landmasses (primarily Africa, Europe, and parts of Asia) and oceans, is enveloped by numerous translucent, light-grey lines representing satellite orbital tracks or coverage swaths. Two small satellites are depicted in orbit, one positioned above the Northern Hemisphere and another below the Southern Hemisphere, each emitting a green, laser-like beam towards the Earth's surface, symbolising active InSAR data collection. In the bottom-right corner, a control panel is visible with the label "Full coverage in days:". Below this, a timeline with numbers "0 1 2 3 4 5 6" indicates the duration. A green highlighted bar extends from "0" to approximately "1", suggesting that full coverage is achieved within one day. This visualisation pertains to the processing strategies for the European Ground Motion Service (EGMS), which relies on InSAR data.
